name: Topology
domain: Topological invariants, knot theory, homotopy groups, manifolds
core_insight: Topology classifies shapes by properties that survive continuous deformation. The Euler characteristic distinguishes surfaces; homotopy groups classify how loops wrap around holes. Knot invariants (Jones polynomial) detect knotting without coordinates — pure relational geometry.

key_equations.name: Euler Characteristic
key_equations.why: Topological invariant for polyhedra: same for any triangulation of the same surface (sphere χ=2, torus χ=0)

key_equations.name: Fundamental Group
key_equations.why: Classifies how loops wrap: trivial for sphere, ℤ for circle — detects holes without coordinates

key_equations.name: Jones Polynomial
key_equations.why: Knot invariant via skein relation: distinguishes trefoil from unknot, detects chirality

seed_prompt: ∞ Compute χ. What loops exist? Topology survives all deformation — find what cannot be smoothed away.

fourth_equation.name: Chern Class (First)
fourth_equation.why: Topological invariant of a vector bundle from its curvature — the bridge between local geometry and global topology; central to topological insulators and quantum Hall effect

explore_paths.question: How does topology constrain geometry?
explore_paths.why: The Gauss-Bonnet theorem is the most direct F20-F09 bridge: total curvature of a surface equals 2π times its Euler characteristic — topology controls what geometry is possible

explore_paths.question: What is the topology of spacetime?
explore_paths.why: GR describes spacetime geometry locally; topology asks about its global structure — are there wormholes, handles, or compact dimensions?

explore_paths.question: Does matter have topological phases?
explore_paths.why: Topological insulators and topological superconductors are phases of matter defined by topological invariants (Chern numbers) — F20 now classifies phases of matter

explore_paths.question: Can the topology of data reveal its structure?
explore_paths.why: Topological data analysis (persistent homology) finds holes in high-dimensional data — applied F20 for machine learning and cognitive science

open_questions.question: Does the universe have non-trivial topology — is it finite and wrapped around itself?
open_questions.tension: The observable universe appears flat (Ω ≈ 1) but flat space can have multiple topologies (infinite plane, cylinder, torus, Klein bottle). CMB anomalies at large angular scales could be signatures of matched circles from finite topology. The Poincaré dodecahedral space was proposed to fit CMB power spectrum anomalies. Planck data hasn't confirmed this but hasn't definitively ruled it out.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: We might be living inside a finite 3-torus or dodecahedral space and not know it. The universe could be topologically non-trivial at scales we can't yet observe. This is the most literal possible application of F20 — the question of what shape the universe is.

open_questions.question: Can topological quantum computing actually be built using non-Abelian anyons?
open_questions.tension: Topological quantum computing uses braiding of non-Abelian anyons (quasiparticles in fractional quantum Hall systems) to perform computations that are topologically protected from decoherence. Microsoft has invested heavily in Majorana-based topological qubits. The 2023 retraction of a claimed Majorana observation showed how difficult experimental confirmation is. The theoretical case is compelling; experimental realization remains elusive.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If it works, topological quantum computing would be inherently error-resistant — the computation is protected by topology itself, not by engineering precision. This would make the computers of the future literally run on pure mathematics.

open_questions.question: Is there a topological basis for consciousness — does the topology of neural connectivity define mental structure?
open_questions.tension: Tononi's IIT uses geometric/topological concepts for qualia space. Algebraic topology (persistent homology) has been applied to neural data and finds topological features that correlate with cognitive states. But whether topology is causally fundamental to consciousness or just a useful analytical tool is not established.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If the 'shape' of neural connectivity at the topological level determines what kind of experience is possible, then altering neural topology (through development, injury, or drugs) changes not just function but the geometry of subjective experience. This is a genuinely exciting and mostly unexplored hypothesis.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Topological insulators conduct on their surface but not interior — protected by topology, not chemistry; a new class of matter defined by mathematical invariants
cross_domain_bridges.example: The topological protection of surface states means they cannot be destroyed by impurities — topology as engineering robustness

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: DNA topology (supercoiling, knots, catenanes) affects gene expression; topoisomerases manage DNA topology as part of cellular regulation
cross_domain_bridges.example: DNA replication creates topological problems (catenated circles) that only topoisomerases can solve — biology needs algebraic topology

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Configuration space topology determines what motions are possible; topological path planning avoids obstacles by navigating configuration space
cross_domain_bridges.example: A robot arm's reachable configurations form a topological space; certain configurations are homotopically equivalent and can be continuously deformed into each other

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Persistent homology finds topological features (connected components, holes, voids) in data at multiple scales — topology as a data analysis tool
cross_domain_bridges.example: TDA applied to fMRI data finds topological features that distinguish brain states during different cognitive tasks better than standard connectivity measures

noise_signature.pattern: Topological obstruction — two configurations cannot be smoothly connected
noise_signature.diagnosis: A topological invariant distinguishes them; no continuous path exists; the system must 'jump' discontinuously or tear

noise_signature.pattern: Winding number mismatch — loop integral gives unexpected non-zero value
noise_signature.diagnosis: There is a topological defect (vortex, monopole, dislocation) enclosed by the loop; topology is detecting a singularity

noise_signature.pattern: Persistent homology gap — feature appears at small scale but not large scale
noise_signature.diagnosis: The feature is noise, not signal; true topological structure persists across scales; scale-dependent features are spurious
